feed.title: SAP Community - ABAP Development
feed.subtitle: ABAP Development Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Confirm Popup with Standard Text (SO10)
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a requirement in ABAP where I need to display a popup window that shows:

  1. A standard text (SO10) that contains more than 300 characters

  2. Additional text (around 10 lines) coming from an internal table

  3. The popup should display both — the standard text followed by the table text

  4. The user should be able to choose Yes or Cancel 

I initially tried using function modules like DISPLAY_TEXT and POPUP_TO_CONFIRM, but:

  • DISPLAY_TEXT supports long text but doesn't allow user interaction

  • POPUP_TO_CONFIRM allows Yes/No but only for short text

 Is there any standard function module to achieve this functionality?

Thanks
Dhaksinamoorthi.

entry.title: send mail delayed
entry.content:
Hey Community!

Is there a way to send a mail to a specified time using CL_BCS?

I did not find any hint where to set a time when the mail should be sent.

Yes, I know, I can program a job that starts to that time and sends the mail, but I wonder if there is a built-in way for this.

Thanks for any hints

~Enno

entry.title: Type for input parameter for XML string
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am trying to define a function module input parameter which will be evaluated to an XML string. Eventually, a web service will be created from this FM.

Can I use the type STRING for the input parameter?

Regards,

Konstantinos

entry.title: Assistance Required: Issues with SCI Check Process and Virus Scan Warning in SAP
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am new to performing SCI checks in the SAP system and am currently facing an issue. Could someone please guide me through the process of performing SCI checks?

While executing the SCI check, I encountered a warning message: "Cannot Perform inspection: Virus scanner cannot be instantiated: Virus scan profile /SMIM_API/Put is not active." Since it is a warning, I proceeded further, but the results showed that no objects were processed, displaying zero. I have attached a snapshot of the result for your reference.

Could anyone advise on how to resolve this warning and ensure the SCI check processes the objects correctly? Any help would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you,
khaza Mohammad

entry.title: Move selection screen parameters data to my table fields
entry.content:
Dear SAP users,

I have a question, I want to copy the values of fields in ly selection-screen to the fields of my custom table.

For the selection screen, I have 

PARAMETERS: p_bukrs like T001-BUKRS OBLIGATORY . 

SELECT-OPTIONS: so_budat FOR ACDOCA-BUDAT OBLIGATORY.

And I have a custom table with fields:

COMPANY_CODE DATA ELEMENT BUKRS

DATE_HIGH DATA ELEMENT DATE

DATE_LOW DATA ELEMENT DATE

Now I want to copy the data of selection screen like this:

COMPANY_CODE =  p_bukrs 

DATE_HIGH = so_budat-high 

DATE_LOW = so_budat-low

I wrote some code, I did a point debug and I found that it come with data for p_bukrs, so_budat-high andso_budat-low but when it come to sy-subrc it give me 4

FORM move_data.
  LOOP AT gt_alv INTO DATA(ls_alv).
    DATA: gt_alv8 TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ztfi_affec_a,
          gs_alv8 TYPE ztfi_affec_a.

    SELECT * FROM ztfi_affec_a INTO TABLE gt_alv8
      WHERE company_code = p_bukrs
        AND date_low >= so_budat-low
        AND date_high <= so_budat-high.

    LOOP AT gt_alv8 INTO gs_alv8.
      gs_alv8-date_high    = so_budat-high.
      gs_alv8-date_low     = so_budat-low.
      gs_alv8-company_code = p_bukrs.
         IF sy-subrc = 0.
           ENDIF.

      SELECT SINGLE * FROM ztfi_affec_a INTO @DATA(ls_exist)
        WHERE company_code = @GS_alv8-company_code
         AND date_high = @GS_alv8-date_high
          AND date_low = @GS_alv8-date_low.

      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        UPDATE ztfi_affec_a FROM gs_alv8.
      ELSE.
        INSERT ztfi_affec_a FROM gs_alv8.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDFORM.

entry.title: What's the future of SAP technical consultant?
entry.content:
With the introduction of next-generation technologies like SAP BTP, ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP), SAP Build (LCNC tools), and SAP Joule (AI), it's clear that SAP is moving towards a clean core, cloud-based future.

However, in reality, many organizations are still operating on legacy ABAP systems, and real-world adoption of these new tools remains limited. With the end of ECC support by 2027, this gap raises some concerns.

❓Key Questions:

1. What is the realistic future for SAP technical consultants in this evolving landscape?

2. Is it practical to invest time in RAP, BTP, or AI tools, given the current slow adoption?

3. Are any real projects or clients actively implementing these technologies?

Would appreciate insights from those currently working on RAP or BTP-based projects or involved in modern SAP implementations.

Thank you!

entry.title: FILTER Operation with NE (Not Equal to) in WHERE Clause not working.
entry.content:
Hello Gurus,

As evident from the subject am trying to make use of FILTER Syntax for finding the Non-matching records between Two Internal tables.

Below is the code snippet,

DATA(lt_delta_spec_chars)
= FILTER #( lt_spec_chars IN lt_material_config
WHERE charact NE charact ).

Below are the snapshots of Data:

CHARACT with value 'CP_SIL_BAND' is the matching and you can see that there is no NON-Matching record,

Still for below code:

The result is:

Requesting to enlighten where I am wrong here.

Thanks,

Sijin

entry.title: SAP EWM ABAP - RF Device Cookie (or any mechanism to track individual devices)
entry.content:
Hi,

What we are trying to achieve is to pair a host name to a printer. No problem with SAP GUI on Windows.

However, when using RF devices, we are only getting IP Address. And each reconnect, same RF gets different IP Address. Regardless if Android or Windows, in my understanding, RF are connected via TELNET. ...So... 🤷‍♂️

So I am thinking, if there are any mechanism to identify each devices? In web architecture we use cookies. This prevents redoing the settings every connect+login. But in SAP world, i don't know how to achieve this in ABAP.

Thanks in advance for any feedback or solution.

I have seen similar dilemma below in our community...

https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-discussions/rf-device-identification/td-p/7474187 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/how-to-check-terminal-name-and-tcode-used-by-specific-user-in-sap-previous/qaq-p/11918303

entry.title: SAP FIORI | Timestamp conversion in fiori front end
entry.content:
Hi SAP  Experts,

I've created a timestamp column in the backend by concatenating the creation date (VBAK-ERDAT) and creation time (VBAK-ERZET). The resulting timestamp is in the format YYYYMMDDHHMMSS, and the values are being generated as expected.

Example:

  • Creation Date: April 25, 2025 (20250425)
  • Creation Time: 5:14:06 AM (051406)
  • Timestamp (backend): 20250425051406

    However, after exposing the CDS view through a Fiori application, the timestamp displayed on the frontend appears to have an additional ~5.5 hours added to it.

    Frontend Example:

    • Timestamp displayed: April 25, 2025, 10:44:06 AM (20250425104406)

      It seems that the SAP Gateway or the Fiori frontend is performing a timezone conversion on the timestamp value.

      As per SAP Note 3215029, this behavior aligns with the standard design. The timestamp shown in UI5 applications may differ from backend values depending on the browser's timezone settings.

      Has anyone else encountered a similar issue? Any suggestions or best practices to handle this discrepancy would be greatly appreciated.

      Best regards,

      Mukhtar

entry.title: UPLOAD DATA FROM EXCEL TO ANY TABLE Z IN SAP
entry.content:
Hello everyone, I'm locking for FM or anyway for check validate field about DATS, TIMS, CURR, CURK,... and Value Range in internal table because I want to upload data from excel to any Z table data. So I need help to validate data before upload into internal table Z*. Here is my code correct and can upload any table Z but it doesn't validate data to internal table.

FORM UPLOAD_DATA_FROM_EXCEL.
  DATA: LT_FILE_TABLE    TYPE FILETABLE,
        LV_RC            TYPE I,
        LV_FILE_PATH     TYPE STRING,
        LV_FILE_LENGTH   TYPE I,
        LV_XSTRING       TYPE XSTRING,
        LT_RAW_DATA      TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF X255,
        LT_EXCEL_DATA    TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ALSMEX_TABLINE,
        LS_EXCEL_LINE    TYPE ALSMEX_TABLINE,
        LT_KEY_FIELDS    TYPE TABLE OF DD03L,
        LV_ROW           TYPE I VALUE 1,
        LV_ERROR_COUNT   TYPE I VALUE 0,
        LV_SUCCESS_COUNT TYPE I VALUE 0,
        LV_MSG           TYPE STRING.

  TYPES: BEGIN OF TY_FIELD_MAPPING,
           EXCEL_NAME TYPE STRING,
           DDIC_NAME  TYPE FIELDNAME,
           COL_POS    TYPE I, " Excel column position
           DATATYPE   TYPE DATATYPE_D, " Add datatype to mapping
         END OF TY_FIELD_MAPPING.

  FIELD-SYMBOLS: <LT_ALV>      TYPE STANDARD TABLE,
                 <LS_ALV>      TYPE ANY,
                 <LS_EXISTING> TYPE ANY,
                 <LV_FIELD>    TYPE ANY,
                 <LV_IS_NEW>   TYPE ANY,
                 <LT_CELLTAB>  TYPE LVC_T_STYL,
                 <LS_CELLTAB>  TYPE LVC_S_STYL,
                 <LV_LOCKED>   TYPE ANY.

  DATA: LT_FILE_CONTENT TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF TEXT255,
        LV_ERROR_TEXT   TYPE STRING,
        LV_MSG_TYPE     TYPE SY-MSGTY,
        LV_MSG_ID       TYPE SY-MSGID,
        LV_MSG_NO       TYPE SY-MSGNO,
        LV_MSG_V1       TYPE SY-MSGV1.

  "-----------------------------------------------------
  " 1. Upload Excel file content
  "-----------------------------------------------------
  CALL METHOD CL_GUI_FRONTEND_SERVICES=>FILE_OPEN_DIALOG
    EXPORTING
      WINDOW_TITLE            = 'Select Excel File'
      FILE_FILTER             = 'Excel Files (*.XLSX)|*.XLSX|'
    CHANGING
      FILE_TABLE              = LT_FILE_TABLE
      RC                      = LV_RC
    EXCEPTIONS
      FILE_OPEN_DIALOG_FAILED = 1
      CNTL_ERROR              = 2
      ERROR_NO_GUI            = 3
      NOT_SUPPORTED_BY_GUI    = 4
      OTHERS                  = 5.

  IF SY-SUBRC <> 0 OR LV_RC = 0 OR LT_FILE_TABLE IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE 'File selection was cancelled' TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE 'W'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  READ TABLE LT_FILE_TABLE INDEX 1 INTO DATA(LS_FILE).
  IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
    MESSAGE 'Could not read selected file information' TYPE 'E'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  LV_FILE_PATH = LS_FILE-FILENAME.

  "-----------------------------------------------------
  " 2. Process Excel content with improved header validation
  "-----------------------------------------------------
  DATA: LV_FILENAME TYPE RLGRAP-FILENAME.
  LV_FILENAME = LV_FILE_PATH.

  " First read only the header row (row 1)
  CALL FUNCTION 'ALSM_EXCEL_TO_INTERNAL_TABLE'
    EXPORTING
      FILENAME                = LV_FILENAME
      I_BEGIN_COL             = 1
      I_BEGIN_ROW             = 1
      I_END_COL               = 100
      I_END_ROW               = 1
    TABLES
      INTERN                  = LT_EXCEL_DATA
    EXCEPTIONS
      INCONSISTENT_PARAMETERS = 1
      UPLOAD_OLE              = 2
      OTHERS                  = 3.

  IF SY-SUBRC <> 0 OR LT_EXCEL_DATA IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE 'Could not read header from Excel file' TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  " Get the expected fields from data dictionary
  DATA: LT_EXPECTED_FIELDS TYPE TABLE OF DFIES,
        LT_ACTUAL_HEADERS  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF STRING,
        LS_HEADER_LINE     TYPE ALSMEX_TABLINE,
        LV_EXPECTED_FIELD  TYPE DFIES-FIELDNAME,
        LV_HEADER_MISSING  TYPE ABAP_BOOL VALUE ABAP_FALSE,
        LT_FIELD_MAPPING   TYPE TABLE OF TY_FIELD_MAPPING,
        LS_FIELD_MAPPING   TYPE TY_FIELD_MAPPING,
        LT_MISSING_FIELDS  TYPE TABLE OF STRING,
        LT_EXTRA_FIELDS    TYPE TABLE OF STRING.

  CALL FUNCTION 'DDIF_FIELDINFO_GET'
    EXPORTING
      TABNAME        = P_TABLE
    TABLES
      DFIES_TAB      = LT_EXPECTED_FIELDS
    EXCEPTIONS
      NOT_FOUND      = 1
      INTERNAL_ERROR = 2
      OTHERS         = 3.

  IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
    MESSAGE 'Could not retrieve expected table fields' TYPE 'E'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

    "-----------------------------------------------------
  " 2. Process Excel content with STRICT header validation
  "-----------------------------------------------------
  DATA:
    lv_error_msg       TYPE string,
    lv_header_mismatch TYPE abap_bool VALUE abap_false.

  " Extract headers from Excel (row 1)
  CLEAR lt_actual_headers.
  LOOP AT lt_excel_data INTO ls_header_line WHERE row = 1.
    IF ls_header_line-value IS INITIAL.
      lv_error_msg = 'Empty header found in column ' && ls_header_line-col && '. All headers must have a value.'.
      lv_header_mismatch = abap_true.
      EXIT.
    ENDIF.
    APPEND ls_header_line-value TO lt_actual_headers.
  ENDLOOP.

  " Check for empty headers
  IF lv_header_mismatch = abap_true.
    MESSAGE lv_error_msg TYPE 'E'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  " Validate number of columns
  IF lines( lt_actual_headers ) <> lines( lt_expected_fields ).
    lv_error_msg = |Number of headers ({ lines( lt_actual_headers ) }) does not match target fields ({ lines( lt_expected_fields ) })|.
    MESSAGE lv_error_msg TYPE 'E'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  " STRICT ORDER VALIDATION - Check each header name and position
  LOOP AT lt_expected_fields INTO DATA(ls_expected_field).
    DATA(lv_index) = sy-tabix.

    " Read corresponding Excel header
    READ TABLE lt_actual_headers INTO DATA(lv_header) INDEX lv_index.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      lv_error_msg = |Header at position { lv_index } is missing|.
      lv_header_mismatch = abap_true.
      EXIT.
    ENDIF.

    " Case-insensitive comparison with exact position check
    IF to_upper( lv_header ) <> to_upper( ls_expected_field-fieldname ).
      lv_error_msg = |Header at position { lv_index } is incorrect. Found: "{ lv_header }", Expected: "{ ls_expected_field-fieldname }"|.
      lv_header_mismatch = abap_true.
      EXIT.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

  " If any mismatch, report error and stop
  IF lv_header_mismatch = abap_true.
    MESSAGE lv_error_msg TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
    EXIT.
  ENDIF.

  " Build field mapping between Excel columns and DDIC fields
  CLEAR LT_EXCEL_DATA.
  " Now read the entire Excel file (starting from row 2 for data)
  CALL FUNCTION 'ALSM_EXCEL_TO_INTERNAL_TABLE'
    EXPORTING
      FILENAME                = LV_FILENAME
      I_BEGIN_COL             = 1
      I_BEGIN_ROW             = 2 " Skip header row
      I_END_COL               = 100
      I_END_ROW               = 9999
    TABLES
      INTERN                  = LT_EXCEL_DATA
    EXCEPTIONS
      INCONSISTENT_PARAMETERS = 1
      UPLOAD_OLE              = 2
      OTHERS                  = 3.

  IF SY-SUBRC <> 0 OR LT_EXCEL_DATA IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE 'Could not read data from Excel file' TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  "-----------------------------------------------------
  " 3. Get key fields from DD03L and also field types for date/time conversion
  SELECT * FROM DD03L INTO TABLE LT_KEY_FIELDS
    WHERE TABNAME = P_TABLE
      AND KEYFLAG = 'X'
      AND AS4LOCAL = 'A'
      AND AS4VERS = '0000'
    ORDER BY POSITION.

  " Get all fields with their data types for date/time conversion
  DATA: LT_FIELD_TYPES TYPE TABLE OF DFIES.
  CALL FUNCTION 'DDIF_FIELDINFO_GET'
    EXPORTING
      TABNAME        = P_TABLE
    TABLES
      DFIES_TAB      = LT_FIELD_TYPES
    EXCEPTIONS
      NOT_FOUND      = 1
      INTERNAL_ERROR = 2
      OTHERS         = 3.

  " 4. Assign ALV data to field symbol
  ASSIGN GT_ALV_DATA->* TO <LT_ALV>.
  IF <LT_ALV> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
    MESSAGE 'Cannot access ALV data' TYPE 'E'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  " 5. Create structure for key comparison
  TYPES: BEGIN OF TY_KEY_STRUCTURE,
           KEY_STRING TYPE STRING,
           ROW_INDEX  TYPE I,
         END OF TY_KEY_STRUCTURE.

  DATA: LT_EXISTING_KEYS TYPE HASHED TABLE OF TY_KEY_STRUCTURE
                         WITH UNIQUE KEY KEY_STRING,
        LT_NEW_KEYS      TYPE HASHED TABLE OF TY_KEY_STRUCTURE
                         WITH UNIQUE KEY KEY_STRING,
        LS_KEY           TYPE TY_KEY_STRUCTURE,
        LT_DUPLICATES    TYPE TABLE OF TY_KEY_STRUCTURE.
  " 6. Process Excel data with date/time conversion
  DATA: LV_CURRENT_ROW TYPE I VALUE 0.
  DATA: LV_DATE_STR       TYPE STRING,
        LV_TIME_STR       TYPE STRING,
        LV_CONVERTED_DATE TYPE STRING,
        LV_CONVERTED_TIME TYPE STRING.

  LOOP AT LT_EXCEL_DATA INTO LS_EXCEL_LINE.
    " New row handling
    IF LS_EXCEL_LINE-ROW <> LV_CURRENT_ROW.
      LV_CURRENT_ROW = LS_EXCEL_LINE-ROW.
      LV_ROW = LV_ROW + 1.

      " Add new line to ALV
      APPEND INITIAL LINE TO <LT_ALV> ASSIGNING <LS_ALV>.

      " Mark as new record
      ASSIGN COMPONENT 'IS_NEW' OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LV_IS_NEW>.
      IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
        <LV_IS_NEW> = ABAP_TRUE.
      ENDIF.

      " Initialize celltab for styling
      ASSIGN COMPONENT 'CELLTAB' OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LT_CELLTAB>.
      IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
        CLEAR <LT_CELLTAB>.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
    ASSIGN COMPONENT LS_EXCEL_LINE-COL OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LV_FIELD>.
    IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
      <LV_FIELD> = LS_EXCEL_LINE-VALUE.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

  " 7. Validate keys for new records and handle duplicates by updating existing records
  DATA: LV_START_INDEX     TYPE I,
        LV_END_INDEX       TYPE I,
        LV_DUPLICATE_FOUND TYPE ABAP_BOOL VALUE ABAP_FALSE,
        LV_KEY_MATCH       TYPE ABAP_BOOL,
        LV_UPDATE_COUNT    TYPE I VALUE 0,
        LV_INSERT_COUNT    TYPE I VALUE 0.

  LV_START_INDEX = LINES( <LT_ALV> ) - LV_ROW + 2.
  LV_END_INDEX = LINES( <LT_ALV> ).

  " First pass: check for duplicate primary keys
  LOOP AT <LT_ALV> ASSIGNING <LS_ALV> FROM LV_START_INDEX TO LV_END_INDEX.
    ASSIGN COMPONENT 'IS_NEW' OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LV_IS_NEW>.
    IF SY-SUBRC = 0 AND <LV_IS_NEW> = ABAP_TRUE.
      " Check for duplicate keys in existing data
      LOOP AT <LT_ALV> ASSIGNING <LS_EXISTING>.
        " Skip new records being added
        IF SY-TABIX BETWEEN LV_START_INDEX AND LV_END_INDEX.
          CONTINUE.
        ENDIF.
        ASSIGN COMPONENT 'IS_DELETED' OF STRUCTURE <LS_EXISTING> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<LV_EXISTING_DELETED>).
        IF SY-SUBRC = 0 AND <LV_EXISTING_DELETED> <> 'X'.
          LV_KEY_MATCH = ABAP_TRUE.

          " Check each key field
          LOOP AT LT_KEY_FIELDS INTO DATA(LS_KEY_FIELD_NEW).
            ASSIGN COMPONENT LS_KEY_FIELD_NEW-FIELDNAME OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LV_FIELD>.
            ASSIGN COMPONENT LS_KEY_FIELD_NEW-FIELDNAME OF STRUCTURE <LS_EXISTING> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<LV_EXISTING_FIELD>).
            IF <LV_FIELD> <> <LV_EXISTING_FIELD>.
              LV_KEY_MATCH = ABAP_FALSE.
              EXIT.
            ENDIF.
          ENDLOOP.

          " If keys match, update the existing record with new data
          IF LV_KEY_MATCH = ABAP_TRUE.
            " Get all non-key fields from the new record
*            LOOP AT LT_FIELD_TYPES INTO DATA(LS_FIELD_TYPE) WHERE KEYFLAG <> 'X'.
*              ASSIGN COMPONENT LS_FIELD_TYPE-FIELDNAME OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LV_FIELD>.
*              IF SY-SUBRC = 0 AND <LV_FIELD> IS NOT INITIAL.
*                " Update the existing record with the new value
*                ASSIGN COMPONENT LS_FIELD_TYPE-FIELDNAME OF STRUCTURE <LS_EXISTING> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<LV_EXISTING_VALUE>).
*                IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
*                  <LV_EXISTING_VALUE> = <LV_FIELD>.
*                ENDIF.
*              ENDIF.
*            ENDLOOP.
            LOOP AT LT_FIELD_TYPES INTO DATA(LS_FIELD_TYPE) WHERE KEYFLAG <> 'X'.
              ASSIGN COMPONENT LS_FIELD_TYPE-FIELDNAME OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LV_FIELD>.
              IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
                " Always update ALV field (even if Excel field is empty)
                ASSIGN COMPONENT LS_FIELD_TYPE-FIELDNAME OF STRUCTURE <LS_EXISTING> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<LV_EXISTING_VALUE>).
                IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
                  <LV_EXISTING_VALUE> = COND #( WHEN <LV_FIELD> IS INITIAL THEN '' ELSE <LV_FIELD> ).
                ENDIF.
              ENDIF.
            ENDLOOP.

            " Mark the new record as not needed (we've updated the existing one)
            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'IS_DELETED' OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LV_FIELD>.
            IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
              <LV_FIELD> = 'X'.
            ENDIF.
            LV_DUPLICATE_FOUND = ABAP_TRUE.
            LV_UPDATE_COUNT = LV_UPDATE_COUNT + 1.
            EXIT. " Exit the inner loop once we've found and updated the matching record
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.

      " If no duplicate found, count as insert
      IF LV_KEY_MATCH = ABAP_FALSE.
        LV_INSERT_COUNT = LV_INSERT_COUNT + 1.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

  " Remove the records marked for deletion (the duplicates we've already processed)
  LOOP AT <LT_ALV> ASSIGNING <LS_ALV>.
    ASSIGN COMPONENT 'IS_DELETED' OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LV_FIELD>.
    IF SY-SUBRC = 0 AND <LV_FIELD> = 'X'.
      DELETE <LT_ALV> INDEX SY-TABIX.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

  " 8. Display results
  IF LV_DUPLICATE_FOUND = ABAP_TRUE.
    LV_MSG = |Processed { LV_ROW - 1 } records: Updated { LV_UPDATE_COUNT } existing records, Added { LV_INSERT_COUNT } new records|.
    MESSAGE LV_MSG TYPE 'S'.
  ELSE.
    LV_MSG = |Successfully added { LV_INSERT_COUNT } new records|.
    MESSAGE LV_MSG TYPE 'S'.
  ENDIF.

  " 9. Refresh ALV display
  GV_DATA_CHANGED = ABAP_TRUE.
  IF GO_GRID IS BOUND.
    CALL METHOD GO_GRID->REFRESH_TABLE_DISPLAY
      EXPORTING
        IS_STABLE = VALUE #( ROW = ABAP_TRUE COL = ABAP_TRUE ).
  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.

entry.title: Eclipse "Install in this IDE" (context of keeping ADT up to date)
entry.content:
I was about to update Eclipse to 2025-06 and install the latest ADT tools. I wanted to try "Install in this IDE" as proposed, for the first time, but what does it mean? After restarting, it still shows Eclipse 2025-03...

The version shows as 2025-03 (menu Help > About Eclipse IDE):

The view "2025-06 release for the X Eclipse IDE for Java Developers is available" still shows up.

NB: I could install ADT 3.50 and it installed the new features correctly (CDS Table Entity support, Ctrl+F/Del/Ctrl+Z bug fixed)

Is it a bug of "Install in this IDE" or did I misunderstand what it is about? Should I really install Eclipse 2025-06 the normal way? (I want to see 2025-06, not 2025-03)

Thanks

Sandra

entry.title: In VA01/VA02, the user exit USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT cannot update the custom fields in VBAK. how do?
entry.content:
In VA01/VA02, the user exit USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT cannot update the custom fields in VBAK. What should I do?

I am using USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT to call a third-party system. After that system returns a value, I need to update the custom field in VBAK with the returned value. However, using UPDATE VBAK doesn't work, and assigning the value directly to XVBAK doesn't work either.

Is there any good solution? I just want to handle this within USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT.

entry.title: Adding Revlv field in ME2M Report
entry.content:
Dear Team,

I adding revlv field in ME2M Report using ME_CHANGE_OUTTAB_CUS~FILL_OUTTAB. Its working fine. but if i click delivery schedule dump error occur. Kindy help me please. Urgent issues in Production

entry.title: how to display attachments like (docx and pdf) based on form id in adobe forms
entry.content: Spoiler

entry.title: Sending notifications inside Fiori after using send object with note
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
In T-code me23n when "send object with note" is used, notification is sent in sap business workplace.
How can this notification be sent in my inbox or push notification in Fiori as well?

entry.title: getting csv file from AL11 into internal table,appending into custom table end field value have #
entry.content: I am uploading csv file into al11 , while debugging i can see in internal table  every line ending with #.  then i am using fm TEXT_CONVERT_TEX_TO_SAP  separate those lines by comma so i can fill internal table data and insert it into custom table , but when i am inserting data into custom table last field has ending value # .  i tried to condense last variable so if extra space will get delete but it didnt helped.

entry.title: Rework Order Component Validation
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

my requirement is validate the components of rework production order based on parent BOM. if given component is not in the BOM (validate the component) will show error like that.

entry.title: How to link screen-painter element to custom table
entry.content:
Hi,

I created an input field using screen painter name 'DATE_AGO' with search helper 'BU_DATE_CHAR', I chose the search helper because I want it like under date format. I have declared in the TOP include a global variable DATA: DATE_AGO TYPE SY-DATUM. 

Now  when I run my project, and I fill that field with date, when I click on enter it automatically clear by it's self, and when I run the debug and I point on the variable name it's empty 00000000.

Here's the affectation of the variable data to the field of my custom table:

 gs_affec_a-date_ago = date_ago.

DATA: DATE_AGO TYPE SY-DATUM.

Please note that I'm not using any clear or something that let the variable be cleared automatically.

entry.title: GOS PNG IN MM03 ERROR
entry.content:
Hi @everyone ,

I am currently working on project where i have to attach png images in mm03 ,

where i was able to attach it with pdf extension but when i tried to attach it as pdf it attach the file but it is corrupted file , anyone have face this before , that will be helpful , please respond as early as possible .

Please refer my previous blog for pdf attachment , i am using same program but i need to attach png/jpeg: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/facing-issue-in-gos-attachment-for-mm03/qaq-p/14139422

Thanks,

Sanket Gode.

entry.title: how to throw message in webdynpro
entry.content:
Hi there,

We are exploring the options to throw warning message in webdynpro.  Below code is given by AI tool to make it possible.  However, we want to implement it in BADI IF_WD_BADI_DOMODIFYVIEW~WDDOMODIFYVIEW. The only parameters available within this BADI method are FIRST_TIME, VIEW, WD_CONTEXT.  How can we get the view controller using these parameters? Thanks!

 

DATA: lo_api_controller TYPE REF TO if_wd_controller,
lo_message_manager TYPE REF TO if_wd_message_manager.
lo_api_controller = wd_this->wd_get_api( ).
lo_message_manager = lo_api_controller->get_message_manager( ).
lo_message_manager->report_warning(
  EXPORTING
message_text = 'This is a warning message.'
).
